Infobip and T-Mobile Integrate Network Apis to Enhance Fraud Protection for Enterprises
data.description:
Infobip is extending security measures for enterprise customers as a go-to-market partner. Now, T-Mobile?s network APIs works seamlessly with Infobip?s platform to enhance user verification with the goal of reducing fraudulent activity. Network APIs make it possible for developers to communicate with various telecom operators to verify device possession and authenticate user identities in-app while delivering the secure and simplified end user experience consumers want.

CAMARA Open Gateway APIs are changing this by establishing a common standard across MNOs, aggregators, and developers, removing a critical barrier and making telecom capabilities far more accessible. By integrating T-Mobile?s advanced network APIs with Infobip?s omnichannel platform, enterprises can deploy a seamless solution that enhances verification and fraud prevention capabilities for partners and end customers.

Wall St opens lower as Middle East uncertainty tempers rally
data.description:
March 24 (Reuters) - Wall Street's main indexes opened lower on Tuesday as renewed doubts over easing Middle East tensions tempered the previous session's relief rally despite President Donald Trump's decision to delay strikes on Iran's power grid.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 108.6 points, or 0.24%, at the open to 46,099.86. The S&P 500 fell 28.9 points, or 0.44%, to 6,552.09, while the Nasdaq Composite dropped 139.2 points, or 0.63%, to 21,807.597.

(Reporting by Purvi Agarwal in Bengaluru; Editing by Maju Samuel)

data.title: Pakistan PM Sharif says ready to host talks between US and Iran
Coalition reaches agreement on Riester pension successor
data.description:
BERLIN (dpa-AFX) - The CDU/CSU and SPD have finalized the details for a new state-subsidized private pension scheme. Higher subsidies than originally planned will be provided for low-income earners with limited savings capacity. Furthermore, self-employed individuals will also be eligible for support, according to announcements made by financial policy spokespeople from both parliamentary groups in Berlin.

Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil (SPD) had submitted an initial reform draft, which underwent several amendments in the Bundestag. For monthly savings of up to 30 euros, a basic allowance of 50 cents per euro saved will be granted. For higher savings amounts up to 150 euros, a basic allowance of 25 cents is planned. The full child supplement of 300 euros per child per year will be triggered starting from a monthly savings contribution of just 25 euros.

The legislation is scheduled for adoption by the Bundestag as early as Thursday. The new pension contracts are expected to be available to consumers starting in January 2027./tam/DP/stw

U.K. Will Stick to Budget Rules In Tackling Energy Crisis, Treasury Chief Says
data.description:
By Paul Hannon


The U.K. government will stick to its budget rules as it responds to the higher energy costs that have followed the U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran, treasury chief Rachel Reeves said Tuesday.

In a statement to lawmakers, Reeves said the government is planning for every contingency, but would not repeat the mistakes of 2022 and 2023, when the support provided to households was "untargeted and unfunded."

"That gave support to the wealthiest of households," she said. "I promise to do what is right and fair."

The government has already announced a modest 53 million pounds ($71.2 million) in support for households reliant on home heating oil, the cost of which has risen sharply.

Worries about the cost of providing wider support has contributed to a sharp rise in yields on U.K. government bonds since the conflict in the Middle East began in late February.

Reeves said the government will stick to its budget rules, which she described as "ironclad."

"I will not put stability at risk," she said.

Reeves said the government will ensure that businesses do not attempt to make windfall profits during the energy crisis triggered by the war by granting additional powers to the Competition and Markets Authority.

"This government will not tolerate any company exploiting this crisis," she said.


Write to Paul Hannon at paul.hannon@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

03-24-26 0920ET

data.title: Gold extends decline on expectations of high interest rates
data.description:
March 24 (Reuters) - Gold prices extended their decline on Tuesday, weighed down by persistent Middle East tensions that fanned worries of inflation and expectations of higher interest rates globally.

Spot gold fell 0.6% to $4,377.93 per ounce by 9:00 a.m. ET (1300 GMT), after hitting its lowest since November at $4,097.99 in the previous session. 

U.S. gold futures for April delivery lost 0.6% to $4,378.80.

"If the war continues and energy prices keep grinding higher, it's not great news for gold," said Bart Melek, global head of commodity strategy at TD Securities.

"Gold is going to be under pressure for the second quarter, but I think by year-end, the gold outlook should again look pretty sweet, as we are hoping that by then central banks like the Fed will have more freedom and we could see the dollar ease and rates drop," he added. [USD/] [US/]

Bullion is considered a safe-haven asset and an inflation hedge, but because it yields no interest, it loses appeal in a high-rate environment.

Iran launched waves of missiles at Israel on Tuesday, a day after U.S. President Donald Trump said there had been "very good and productive" talks aiming at halting the war unleashed by the U.S. and Israel now raging across the Middle East.

The war has effectively halted shipments of about one-fifth of the world's oil and liquefied natural gas through the Strait of Hormuz, pushing up energy prices and stoking inflation concerns. Following this, top central banks have emphasised their readiness to act if the war drives a broader surge in prices. [O/R]

Spot gold has fallen about 22% from its record peak of $5,594.82 reached on January 29.

"The recent price slump is likely to be just as much of an overreaction as the massive rise at the start of the year. In a sense, the pendulum has swung from one extreme to the other for gold," analysts at Commerzbank said in a note.

Among other metals, spot silver fell 1.3% to $68.20, platinum added 0.2% to $1,885.18 and palladium lost 2.9% to $1,392.25.

(Reporting by Ashitha Shivaprasad in Bengaluru; Editing by Sahal Muhammed)

By Ashitha Shivaprasad

data.title: Atlas Copco to buy SDC Division assets from CVD Equipment Corporation
Canada Factory Sales Climb Estimated 3.8% in February
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart


OTTAWA--Factory activity in Canada rebounded last month, likely shaking off the slump in auto sales to start the year when vehicle plants in Ontario were shuttered for retooling.

Manufacturing shipments increase an estimated 3.8% in February from a month prior, Statistics Canada said Tuesday. That would mark the strongest advance in month and a recovery after recent weakness for an industry that has been hit hard by the fallout from the shift in U.S. trade policy and tariffs.

Statistics Canada said the largest increases in February were recorded in transportation equipment and food.

Statistics Canada's sales projection for last month was based on a weighted response rate to its monthly survey of roughly 71%. The official survey results, based on more complete data, are scheduled to be released April 15.

Factory sales in the country dropped 3% January to the lowest level in eight months, in part due to extended winter shutdowns at several auto plants to complete retooling for new models and line maintenance. Sales measured in volume terms were down a steeper 3.9% for the month and 6.2% year-over-year.

The rebound hints at a stronger footing for the economy as businesses and consumers brace for supply chain disruption and increased energy costs amid the ongoing war in the Middle East, which has driven up crude oil prices and costs at the pump.

Manufacturing and other trade-exposed segments of Canada's economy had a rough year in 2025 thanks to the Trump administration's tariffs and lingering uncertainty as the existing free trade pact between Canada, the U.S. and Mexico comes up for renegotiation this year.

S&P Global's monthly manufacturing purchasing managers' survey for a second monthly running signaled a pick up for the industry in February, with rising volumes of new work and an increase in employment. The headline index nudged up to 51 from January's 50.4, positive back-to-back readings following 11 straight months below the 50 mark separating contraction from expansion.


Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

03-24-26 0915ET

Assa Abloy proposes two new board members
data.description:
Shareholders of the lock manufacturer Assa Abloy are invited to the Annual General Meeting on Tuesday, April 28, in Stockholm.

The Board of Directors proposes a dividend of SEK 6.40 per share, to be paid in two semi-annual installments of SEK 3.20 per share.

The Nomination Committee proposes the election of Astrid Mozes and Jurgen Timperman as new members of the Board.

Astrid Mozes has an extensive background in senior executive roles at Danfoss and Eaton.

Jurgen Timperman is currently the Chief Operating Officer at Encore Fire Protection and has previously held leadership positions at Carrier Global and United Technologies.

The Committee proposes the re-election of Chairman Johan Hjertonsson, Vice Chairman Carl Douglas, and board members Erik Ekudden, Sofia Schörling Högberg, Lena Olving, Victoria Van Camp, and Susanne Pahlén Åklundh.

The Board seeks authorization from the AGM to resolve on the acquisition and transfer of the company's own shares.

Furthermore, the AGM is proposed to approve an incentive program for senior executives and key employees, comprising a maximum of 830,000 Class B shares.

Bank of Montreal Teams With CME Group, Google Cloud on Tokenized Cash Platform
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart


Bank of Montreal plans to launch tokenized cash capabilities that will allow institutional clients to move funds securely at all times.

The Canadian bank will offer 24/7 tokenized cash services using derivatives marketplace CME Group's network on Google Cloud Universal Ledger. The result aims to be a bank-anchored framework that will enable clients to convert dollars into a tokenized instrument for use with margined products at CME.

Bank of Montreal said it will be the first bank to offer CME's tokenized cash solution on Google Cloud Universal Ledger.

Tokenized cash represents currency or bank deposits as digital tokens on a distributed ledger or blockchain, promising instant, programmable settlements.

Momentum around tokenized money is building.

Deloitte in a report this month said stablecoins and tokenized deposits are poised to reshape global finance, and that Canadian banks must modernize infrastructure, define their roles and build strategies to navigate evolving regulatory changes and new market demands. Stablecoins alone currently have a market capitalization exceeding 400 billion Canadian dollars (US$291.5 billion) as of February, growing by roughly a multiple of 10 since the end of 2020, Deloitte said.

Bank of Montreal, which ranks as one of the largest banks in North America by assets, said it is expanding use cases for accessing tokenized cash. CME's tokenized cash solution is designed to support high-value, real-time settlement needs for institutional participants, it added.

The bank said it plans to offer an institutional settlement instrument to regulated financial-services firms operating in capital markets and the commercial-banking space in the second half of 2026, pending regulatory approval. The service will be available to mutual clients of the bank and CME.

The instrument will allow Bank of Montreal to offer traditional commercial-bank funds in digital form, made available to a broader set of BMO clients for business-to-business payments, treasury movements, and programmable cash applications.

Bank clients will be able to convert U.S. dollars into tokenized deposits and tokenized cash 24/7, which Bank of Montreal said will allow it to meet market needs such as extended trading hours, more continuous operations and moving collateral without traditional cutoff constraints.


Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

03-24-26 0909ET
